### Post by GCoffee on 2008-06-10
Hello,

I installed Fedora 9 yesterday, that means my total installed OS' go up to three, windows, ubuntu and fedora. Now, In the setup It asked me about what OS' i would like in grub, and I added the partition with ubuntu on.

Only to find out that I get some error 13 - not recognisable exectuable file when trying to load, here's a copy of my grub file in fedora:

# grub.conf generated by anaconda
#
# Note that you do not have to rerun grub after making changes to this file
# NOTICE:  You do not have a /boot partition.  This means that
#          all kernel and initrd paths are relative to /, eg.
#          root (hd0,4)
#          kernel /boot/vmlinuz-version ro root=/dev/sda5
#          initrd /boot/initrd-version.img
#boot=/dev/sda
default=0
timeout=5
splashimage=(hd0,4)/boot/grub/splash.xpm.gz
hiddenmenu
title Fedora (2.6.25-14.fc9.i686)
	root (hd0,4)
	kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.25-14.fc9.i686 ro root=UUID=e2608cba-9757-4b99-8d8b-49f7f788d4af rhgb quiet
	initrd /boot/initrd-2.6.25-14.fc9.i686.img
title Ubuntu 8.04 Hardy Heron
	rootnoverify (hd0,2)
	chainloader +1
title Windows XP Home Edition
	rootnoverify (hd0,0)
	chainloader +1


BTW, Windows does load...

Please help,

Cheers,
GCoffee.

---

### Post by hal8000 on 2008-06-10
Next time you add a new linux system, choose not to install grub or a bootloader.
Then create a mountpoint, mount the root file system and change to boot/grub on the newly installed system. Copy the lines from menu.lst and edit the hosts menu.lst, in your case adding the lines to ubuntu menu.lst would boot fedora:

title Fedora (2.6.25-14.fc9.i686)
root (hd0,4)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.25-14.fc9.i686 ro root=UUID=e2608cba-9757-4b99-8d8b-49f7f788d4af rhgb quiet
initrd /boot/initrd-2.6.25-14.fc9.i686.img

To your problem, the fedora grub is chainloading and its telling me that windows is the primary first partition, and that Ubuntu has a single / partition on partition 3.


I dont know why windows fails to loads, maybe you want to try makeactive above the chainloader +1.

To verify partitions, boot from the Hardy live cd and post the output of
sudo fdisk -l

---
